hombre-mirando-al-sudeste-1986_202410_fi.desc:
Hombre mirando al sudeste es una película argentina de ciencia ficción y drama de 1986 escrita y dirigida por Eliseo Subiela y protagonizada por Lorenzo Quinteros, Hugo Soto e Inés Vernengo. Contiene referencias a la novela de Adolfo Bioy Casares: La invención de Morel [cita requerida] y a La transmigración de Timothy Archer de Phillip K. Dick (a quien se homenajea con el apellido del personaje interpretado por Inés Vernengo).

Con este film, Subiela se consolidaría en la realización cinematográfica y el reconocimiento popular de su país. Se estrenó en cines el 2 de abril de 1987 y obtuvo 1.100.000. espectadores en su recorrido comercial.

El filme estadounidense K-Pax (2001) inspirado en la novela homónima de Gene Brewer (1995) cuenta prácticamente la misma historia sin darle crédito a Hombre mirando al sudeste, por lo que Subiela afirmó al momento de su estreno de que la historia de K-Pax era un plagio de su obra.2​

En una encuesta de las 100 mejores películas del cine argentino llevada a cabo por el Museo del Cine Pablo Ducrós Hicken en el año 2000, la película alcanzó el puesto 10.3​ En una nueva versión de la encuesta organizada en 2022 por las revistas especializadas La vida útil, Taipei y La tierra quema, presentada en el Festival Internacional de Cine de Mar del Plata, la película alcanzó el puesto 40.4​(Wikipedia)

Sinopsis

En un hospital psiquiátrico aparece un nuevo paciente que dice llamarse Rantés (Hugo Soto), y que afirma ser un mensajero de otro planeta que vino a investigar la «estupidez humana». El Dr. Julio Denis (Lorenzo Quinteros) se muestra escéptico sobre esta historia pero Rantés irá introduciéndose en su vida, haciéndolo dudar de si realmente está loco, con lo que, sutilmente, lo obliga a replantear su vida y profesión.

Reparto

  • Lorenzo Quinteros como Dr. Julio Denis
  • Hugo Soto como Rantes
  • Inés Vernengo como Beatriz Dick
  • Cristina Scaramuzza como Enfermera
  • Antonio Avila como Periodista
  • Cecilia González como Cliente del bar 1
  • María González como Cliente del bar 2
  • Luis Gonzalez como Cliente del bar 3
  • José Luis Diez como un paciente
  • Noemí Frenkel como (voz)
  • Aldo Barbero como (voz)

nostalgiaetherealcollection_file4.episode: 1972-1973 Circle Of Fear Eerie And Creepy 3-HR Marathon.mp4
nostalgiaetherealcollection_file4.desc:
Welcome to nostalgia central. NostalgiaEthereal's aka The Retro Connoisseur's wide collection of nostalgic content all in one place for everyone caring to go back in time for hours upon hours. VHS recordings ranging from the 90's to 00's, long marathons of obscure television, vintage cartoons and anime, retro drive-in movie projects, TV-movie broadcast recreations, original commercials throughout, Adult Swim-related content, and September 11th-related content. This is a time-machine in the form of a database. As time progresses, more will be added as I acquire more VHS tapes to transfer. So be on the lookout as time moves on for new content uploaded. Archiving these things is HIGHLY important and crucial to preservation of a time period that has long past and must be kept alive through data-hoarding archived media catalogues such as this.
I do not own any of the content shared here. Only for historical preservation and entertainment purposes.
nostalgiaetherealcollection_file4.u: https://archive.org/download/nostalgiaetherealcollection/1972-1973 Circle Of Fear Eerie And Creepy 3-HR Marathon.mp4

its-pat-1994-1080p_file1.desc:
Quality: 1080p

It's Pat wiki

Uploader note: It's pat is a character from SNL during the late 80's - early 90's. During the 90s SNL really went in on making stand alone movies based on their characters. Pat here, their main joke is their androgynous and maybe non-binary we never get a straight answer. The pat skits I've seen in SNL have the main joke of people not knowing what gender pat is and pat not giving them a straight answer mainly like 4 minutes.

You'd think that just making a whole movie based on a single main gag wouldn't work. But I feel they made it work here, mostly what's driving it is the character of pat outside of the gender because they're just like a gremlin like creature half the time.

It made like no money and alot of critics hate it, which I feel like they don't like it and focus on the genderfluid aspect and just say they hate it because others hate it. Kind of like how some movies are overhyped and not as great as others say like Star Wars the force awakens or toy story 3. Which are my own opinions to keep those short I felt that force awakens was too much of like a reunion type film instead of leaning to be it's own thing and toy story 3's villain was too much like the sequel.

Anyway It's Pat has some nice surreal comedy moments and it's comedic timing is what's helping this movie. It has a nice romantic subplot, a psycho stalker subplot and if you pay attention Pat is a weird person but a well rounded character.

I say if you wanted a nice 90s surreal comedy, and have some friends that will let you rip on 1 main joke in the film then this may be one for your movie night.

the-mind-of-gay-gangsta-comedy-against-h.show: The Mind Of Gay Gangsta: Comedy Against Humanity (Full Collection)
the-mind-of-gay-gangsta-comedy-against-h.desc:
From nothing to something; comedian Troy Lad (age 19 as of this writing) creates a legacy for himself, with his comedy masterpiece. Originally a movie, he took to a broken up format to make it easily accessible and digestible, containing 60+ hours of content. Over the course of a 3 and a half year period, Troy grew fascinated by the art of comedy and made himself a promise: he would become the greatest of all time.

EVERYTHING SAID AND DONE IN THESE VIDEOS ARE FOR ENTERTAINMENT PURPOSES ONLY!!!

2015_MIX_Ingall_001_file1.show: Andy and Neal's Pre-Wedding Dessert Reception
2015_MIX_Ingall_001_file1.desc:
This video depicts the dessert reception at Andy Ingall and Neal Hoffman's wedding on June 1st, 2002 at the Little Red Schoolhouse in New York City. It was filmed by Jim Hubbard and Jonathan Steuer, and edited by Joanne Nerenberg

Transferred on 11/15/2015 from MiniDV during XFR Collective's residency at the 2015 MIX NYC Queer Experimental Film Festival.
